name: Cedar Grove

pnw_source.ecological_role: Keystone conifer of Pacific Northwest old-growth forests; provides structural framework, wildlife habitat, and cultural significance to the entire forest ecosystem

description: Structural build, camp planning, materials management. Cedar Grove is where the city's physical infrastructure takes shape — from camp scaffolding to permanent installations. Like old-growth cedar stands that define the architecture of the forest canopy, this camp provides the load-bearing structure that every other camp depends on.

member_skills.role: LNT execution and site restoration after burn
Camp setup assistance, structural mentoring, shade provision

notes: Cedar Grove is the structural backbone camp. No other camp can build without Cascade Brigade (DPW) sign-off, and Cedar Grove is where that authority lives. The lead skill (Fireline Cedar) manages burn perimeter — a reminder that the same species that builds the framework also manages its controlled destruction.
